message:
🌱 cert-manager: Wildcard certificates support (#1844)

* 🌱 Add cert-manager: Wildcard certificates support mission

* 🌱 Add cert-manager: Wildcard certificates support mission

Signed-off-by: github-actions[bot] <41898282+github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Signed-off-by: github-actions[bot] <41898282+github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: github-actions[bot] <41898282+github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,75 @@
+{
+  "version": "kc-mission-v1",
+  "name": "cert-manager-273-wildcard-certificates-support",
+  "missionClass": "fixer",
+  "author": "KubeStellar Bot",
+  "authorGithub": "kubestellar",
+  "mission": {
+    "title": "cert-manager: Wildcard certificates support",
+    "description": "Wildcard certificates support. Requested by 26+ users.",
+    "type": "feature",
+    "status": "completed",
+    "steps": [
+      {
+        "title": "Check current cert-manager deployment",
+        "description": "Verify your cert-manager version and configuration:\n```bash\nkubectl get pods -n cert-manager -l app.kubernetes.io/name=cert-manager\nhelm list -n cert-manager 2>/dev/null || echo \"Not installed via Helm\"\n```\nThis feature requires a working cert-manager installation."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Review cert-manager configuration",
+        "description": "Inspect the relevant cert-manager configuration:\n```bash\nkubectl get all -n cert-manager -l app.kubernetes.io/name=cert-manager\nkubectl get configmap -n cert-manager -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=cert-manager\n```\nAre there any plans to support wildcard certificates?\n\nhttps://letsencrypt.org/2017/07/06/wildcard-certificates-coming-jan-2018.html"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Apply the fix for Wildcard certificates support",
+        "description": "Adds support for the ACME v2 protocol in favour of v1\nI've updated the PR to use Pebble instead of Boulder during e2e tests\n\nI have also copied across the upcoming `golang.org/x/crypto/acme/v2` package from the gerrit codereview so we can get ahead of the curve on testing this:\n```yaml\nCertificate:\n    Data:\n        Version: 3 (0x2)\n        Serial Number:\n            fa:f5:cc:92:f2:02:15:58:37:05:02:9f:bf:53:9f:9c:d4:0c\n    Signature Algorithm: sha256WithRSAEncryption\n        Issuer: CN=Fake LE Intermediate X1\n        Validity\n            Not Before: Mar 23 15:56:39 2018 GMT\n            Not After : Jun 21 15:56:39 2018 GMT\n        Subject: CN=*.james-gcp.redacted.net\n        Subject Public Key Info:\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Verify the feature works",
+        "description": "Test that the new capability is working as expected:\n```bash\nkubectl get pods -n cert-manager -l app.kubernetes.io/name=cert-manager\nkubectl get events -n cert-manager --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10\n```\nConfirm the feature described in \"Wildcard certificates support\" is functioning correctly."
+      }
+    ],
+    "resolution": {
+      "summary": "Adds support for the ACME v2 protocol in favour of v1\nI've updated the PR to use Pebble instead of Boulder during e2e tests\n\nI have also copied across the upcoming `golang.org/x/crypto/acme/v2` package from the gerrit codereview so we can get ahead of the curve on testing this: https://go-review.googlesource.com/c/crypto/+/86635\n\n~I have also not tested whether it is possible to obtain wildcard",
+      "codeSnippets": [
+        "Certificate:\n    Data:\n        Version: 3 (0x2)\n        Serial Number:\n            fa:f5:cc:92:f2:02:15:58:37:05:02:9f:bf:53:9f:9c:d4:0c\n    Signature Algorithm: sha256WithRSAEncryption\n        Issuer: CN=Fake LE Intermediate X1\n        Validity\n            Not Before: Mar 23 15:56:39 2018 GMT\n            Not After : Jun 21 15:56:39 2018 GMT\n        Subject: CN=*.james-gcp.redacted.net\n        Subject Public Key Info:"
+      ]
+    }
+  },
+  "metadata": {
+    "tags": [
+      "cert-manager",
+      "graduated",
+      "security",
+      "feature"
+    ],
+    "cncfProjects": [
+      "cert-manager"
+    ],
+    "targetResourceKinds": [],
+    "difficulty": "intermediate",
+    "issueTypes": [
+      "feature"
+    ],
+    "maturity": "graduated",
+    "sourceUrls": {
+      "issue": "https://github.com/cert-manager/cert-manager/issues/273",
+      "repo": "https://github.com/cert-manager/cert-manager",
+      "pr": "https://github.com/cert-manager/cert-manager/pull/309"
+    },
+    "reactions": 26,
+    "comments": 17,
+    "synthesizedBy": "copilot"
+  },
+  "prerequisites": {
+    "kubernetes": ">=1.24",
+    "tools": [
+      "kubectl"
+    ],
+    "description": "A running Kubernetes cluster with cert-manager installed or the issue environment reproducible."
+  },
+  "security": {
+    "scannedAt": "2026-04-01T06:38:12.818Z",
+    "scannerVersion": "cncf-gen-3.0.0",
+    "sanitized": true,
+    "findings": []
+  }
+}

+    "status": "completed",
+    "steps": [
+      {
+        "title": "Check current cert-manager deployment",
+        "description": "Verify your cert-manager version and configuration:\n```bash\nkubectl get pods -n cert-manager -l app.kubernetes.io/name=cert-manager\nhelm list -n cert-manager 2>/dev/null || echo \"Not installed via Helm\"\n```\nThis feature requires a working cert-manager installation."
+      },

+        "description": "Inspect the relevant cert-manager configuration:\n```bash\nkubectl get all -n cert-manager -l app.kubernetes.io/name=cert-manager\nkubectl get configmap -n cert-manager -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=cert-manager\n```\nAre there any plans to support wildcard certificates?\n\nhttps://letsencrypt.org/2017/07/06/wildcard-certificates-coming-jan-2018.html"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Apply the fix for Wildcard certificates support",
+        "description": ":\n\nAdds support for the ACME v2 protocol in favour of v1\n\n:\n\n~This PR is not ready\n\n. We need to validate and ensure boulder runs with the v2 protocol enabled during our e2e tests. In future we can switch to something like [pebble](https://github.com/letsencrypt/pebble), a lightweight alternative\n```yaml\nCertificate:\n    Data:\n        Version: 3 (0x2)\n        Serial Number:\n            fa:f5:cc:92:f2:02:15:58:37:05:02:9f:bf:53:9f:9c:d4:0c\n    Signature Algorithm: sha256WithRSAEncryption\n        Issuer: CN=Fake LE Intermediate X1\n        Validity\n            Not Before: Mar 23 15:56:39 2018 GMT\n            Not After : Jun 21 15:56:39 2018 GMT\n        Subject: CN=*.james-gcp.redacted.net\n        Subject Public Key Info:\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Verify the feature works",
+        "description": "Test that the new capability is working as expected:\n```bash\nkubectl get pods -n cert-manager -l app.kubernetes.io/name=cert-manager\nkubectl get events -n cert-manager --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10\n```\nConfirm the feature described in \"Wildcard certificates support\" is functioning correctly."
+      }
+    ],
+    "resolution": {
+      "summary": ":\n\nAdds support for the ACME v2 protocol in favour of v1\n\n:\n\n~This PR is not ready\n\n. We need to validate and ensure boulder runs with the v2 protocol enabled during our e2e tests.",
+      "codeSnippets": [
+        "Certificate:\n    Data:\n        Version: 3 (0x2)\n        Serial Number:\n            fa:f5:cc:92:f2:02:15:58:37:05:02:9f:bf:53:9f:9c:d4:0c\n    Signature Algorithm: sha256WithRSAEncryption\n        Issuer: CN=Fake LE Intermediate X1\n        Validity\n            Not Before: Mar 23 15:56:39 2018 GMT\n            Not After : Jun 21 15:56:39 2018 GMT\n        Subject: CN=*.james-gcp.redacted.net\n        Subject Public Key Info:"
+      ]
+    }
